name: Corsican Cheesecake (Fiadone)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F. Blanch lemon zest in boiling water briefly and plunge into cold water; drain well.
2. Beat egg yolks, sugar, and vanilla extract in a bowl of an electric mixer at high speed until thick and lemon-colored, about 3 minutes.
3. Cream ricotta cheese in a food processor fitted with a metal blade until smooth. Gradually incorporate the ricotta and lemon zest into the egg yolk mixture, beating on low speed.
4. Beat egg whites in a bowl of an electric mixer at top speed until stiff but not dry. By hand, whisk 1/3 of the egg whites into the cheese mixture and combine thoroughly.
5. Gently fold in the remaining egg whites with a rubber spatula. Do not overmix, but be sure that the mixture is well blended and no streaks of white remain.
6. Pour batter into a buttered and floured 9-inch springform pan. Place pan in the center of the oven; bake until the cheesecake is a deep golden brown, pulls away from the sides of the pan, and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean, about 1 hour and 30 minutes.
7. Transfer to a rack to cool. Once cooled, cover cheesecake with plastic wrap; refrigerate. (The cake can be made up to 1 day in advance.)
8. To serve, release sides of springform pan, leaving the cheesecake on the pan base. Sprinkle top with confectioners' sugar and garnish with fresh fruit.
